title: Who dared to dive deep into the ocean?
description: A history web about exploring the deep ocean. Historical = supported by evidence. Speculative = educated guess. Alternate = what-if history.

narrator.name: Captain Current
narrator.intro: Read the clue, make a brave guess, then reveal the branch.
wrongLines: Good guess, but history took another path.
Not quite. Test the next clue.

missions.title: Machines Below
missions.description: Follow inventions that let humans stay underwater longer.

missions.title: Into the Abyss
missions.description: Travel from submarines to trench explorers.

deep-ocean-exploration::root.title: Who dared to dive deep into the ocean?
deep-ocean-exploration::root.summary:
Humans have crossed oceans for thousands of years.
But the deep ocean stayed mysterious and dark.
How did people first explore what lay below?

prediction.question: What came first in ocean exploration?

choices.feedback: Ancient divers explored long before machines.

powerUp.title: Timeline Sense
powerUp.text: Put inventions in the right historical order.

deep-ocean-exploration::ancient-divers.title: Ancient Free Divers
deep-ocean-exploration::ancient-divers.summary:
Thousands of years ago, divers collected food, sponges, and treasure.
They held their breath and relied on skill.
Which goal pushed diving further?

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: Notice how work and trade drive invention.

deep-ocean-exploration::ancient-curiosit.title: Mysteries Beneath Ships
deep-ocean-exploration::ancient-curiosit.summary:
Sailors wondered what lived under the waves.
Stories mixed truth and imagination.
Could people ever stay underwater longer?

powerUp.title: Question Builder
powerUp.text: Big discoveries often start with curiosity.

deep-ocean-exploration::pearl-divers.title: Pearl Divers
deep-ocean-exploration::pearl-divers.summary:
Divers searched for valuable pearls.
Some trained from childhood.
Their success showed how much humans could do underwater.
deep-ocean-exploration::pearl-divers.consequences: Created demand for better diving methods.

powerUp.title: Economic Clue
powerUp.text: Money and trade can accelerate exploration.

deep-ocean-exploration::sponge-divers.title: Sponge Divers
deep-ocean-exploration::sponge-divers.summary:
Divers gathered sea sponges for cleaning and trade.
The work was dangerous.
People wanted safer ways to stay underwater.

powerUp.title: Work and Technology
powerUp.text: Jobs often inspire inventions.

deep-ocean-exploration::diving-bell-idea.title: The Bell Idea
deep-ocean-exploration::diving-bell-idea.summary:
People noticed trapped air could remain underwater.
Could a container carry air beneath the sea?

powerUp.title: Pattern Finder
powerUp.text: Observe nature to invent tools.

deep-ocean-exploration::diving-bell.title: The Diving Bell
deep-ocean-exploration::diving-bell.summary:
A bell-shaped chamber trapped air underwater.
Divers could breathe for a time inside it.
How could it be improved?

prediction.question: What made diving bells more useful?

powerUp.title: Engineering Lens
powerUp.text: Small improvements can transform a tool.

deep-ocean-exploration::halley-bell.title: Edmond Halley's Bell
deep-ocean-exploration::halley-bell.summary:
In the 1690s, Halley improved the diving bell.
Fresh air barrels extended time underwater.

powerUp.title: Innovation Trail
powerUp.text: Track how one invention improves another.

deep-ocean-exploration::salvage-work.title: Recovering Shipwrecks
deep-ocean-exploration::salvage-work.summary:
Divers searched sunken ships for cargo.
This work funded better equipment.

powerUp.title: Cause and Effect
powerUp.text: Resources help technology advance.

deep-ocean-exploration::air-pumps.title: Air Pumps and Helmets
deep-ocean-exploration::air-pumps.summary:
Engineers developed ways to send air to divers.
Heavy helmets appeared.
Would freedom or safety matter more next?

powerUp.title: Tradeoff Tracker
powerUp.text: Technology often balances competing goals.

deep-ocean-exploration::deep-work.title: Working Below the Waves
deep-ocean-exploration::deep-work.summary:
Construction, salvage, and science all needed divers.
Demand for deeper work kept growing.

powerUp.title: Need Drives Change
powerUp.text: Identify the problem inventors wanted to solve.

deep-ocean-exploration::surface-suits.title: Surface-Supplied Diving Suits
deep-ocean-exploration::surface-suits.summary:
Divers wore helmets connected to air hoses.
They could work deeper than before.
deep-ocean-exploration::surface-suits.consequences: Opened more of the underwater world.

powerUp.title: Technology Ladder
powerUp.text: New inventions build on earlier ones.

deep-ocean-exploration::portable-air.title: The Dream of Portable Air
deep-ocean-exploration::portable-air.summary:
Divers wanted to move freely.
Could they carry their own air supply?

powerUp.title: Problem Solver
powerUp.text: Define the next challenge clearly.

deep-ocean-exploration::scuba.title: Scuba and Cousteau
deep-ocean-exploration::scuba.summary:
Jacques Cousteau and Émile Gagnan helped create the Aqua-Lung.
Divers could swim freely with compressed air.
What changed most?

powerUp.title: Turning Point
powerUp.text: Recognize a breakthrough invention.

deep-ocean-exploration::underwater-films.title: Underwater Films
deep-ocean-exploration::underwater-films.summary:
Cousteau showed ocean life to millions.
People became fascinated by the sea.
deep-ocean-exploration::underwater-films.consequences: Built public interest in ocean science.

powerUp.title: Media Detective
powerUp.text: Ideas spread through communication.

deep-ocean-exploration::scientific-divin.title: Scientific Diving
deep-ocean-exploration::scientific-divin.summary:
Researchers could now observe marine life directly.
New discoveries arrived quickly.

powerUp.title: Field Science
powerUp.text: Observe nature directly whenever possible.

deep-ocean-exploration::ocean-interest.title: A Growing Ocean Age
deep-ocean-exploration::ocean-interest.summary:
More people wanted to explore deeper waters.
Divers still had limits.
What could go deeper than a diver?

powerUp.title: Next Question
powerUp.text: Each solution reveals a new challenge.

deep-ocean-exploration::bathysphere.title: The Bathysphere
deep-ocean-exploration::bathysphere.summary:
William Beebe and Otis Barton descended in a steel sphere.
They saw deep-sea life few humans had witnessed.
deep-ocean-exploration::bathysphere.consequences: Proved humans could safely visit greater depths.

powerUp.title: Observation Skill
powerUp.text: Direct observation can change science.

deep-ocean-exploration::submarine-path.title: Submarine Technology
deep-ocean-exploration::submarine-path.summary:
Submarines demonstrated that humans could survive deep underwater.
Engineers dreamed of going even deeper.

powerUp.title: Connection Maker
powerUp.text: Different technologies can support each other.

deep-ocean-exploration::deep-observation.title: Creatures of the Deep
deep-ocean-exploration::deep-observation.summary:
Scientists found strange animals living in darkness.
Life existed where many thought it could not.

powerUp.title: Evidence First
powerUp.text: Nature may surprise our expectations.

deep-ocean-exploration::trieste.title: Trieste Reaches Challenger Deep
deep-ocean-exploration::trieste.summary:
In 1960, Trieste descended to the deepest known part of the ocean.
Humans finally reached the bottom of Challenger Deep.

powerUp.title: Extreme Environments
powerUp.text: Learn how scientists face harsh conditions.

deep-ocean-exploration::deep-sea-science.title: A New Science Frontier
deep-ocean-exploration::deep-sea-science.summary:
Deep oceans became laboratories.
Scientists studied geology, biology, and chemistry.

powerUp.title: Big Picture
powerUp.text: Different sciences can work together.

deep-ocean-exploration::deep-challenges.title: Crushing Pressure
deep-ocean-exploration::deep-challenges.summary:
Deep ocean pressure can crush ordinary machines.
Engineers needed stronger designs.

powerUp.title: Constraint Finder
powerUp.text: Understand the limits of a system.

deep-ocean-exploration::mapping-trench.title: Mapping the Mariana Trench
deep-ocean-exploration::mapping-trench.summary:
Ships used sonar to map the deep seafloor.
The Mariana Trench became one of Earth's best-known deep-ocean features.
What tool helped most?
deep-ocean-exploration::mapping-trench.consequences: Revealed](https://oceanexplorer.noaa.gov%22}],%22consequences%22:%22Revealed) the shape of the deep ocean.

powerUp.title: Map Reader
powerUp.text: Maps reveal hidden places.

deep-ocean-exploration::sonar.title: Sound Maps the Seafloor
deep-ocean-exploration::sonar.summary:
Sonar sends sound waves and measures echoes.
It can map places humans cannot see.

powerUp.title: Indirect Evidence
powerUp.text: Scientists often measure clues instead of seeing directly.

deep-ocean-exploration::robots.title: Robots Go Deeper
deep-ocean-exploration::robots.summary:
Remotely operated vehicles and autonomous robots explore dangerous depths.
They can stay underwater much longer than humans.

powerUp.title: Tool Choice
powerUp.text: Choose the right tool for the job.

deep-ocean-exploration::modern-maps.title: Modern Ocean Maps
deep-ocean-exploration::modern-maps.summary:
Scientists combine sonar, satellites, and robots.
The ocean is mapped more accurately every year.

powerUp.title: Data Combiner
powerUp.text: Many kinds of evidence work together.

deep-ocean-exploration::ocean-secrets.title: Many Secrets Remain
deep-ocean-exploration::ocean-secrets.summary:
Large parts of the ocean remain poorly explored.
The deep sea still holds surprises.

powerUp.title: Open Questions
powerUp.text: Science is never completely finished.

deep-ocean-exploration::future-ocean.title: Future Ocean Exploration
deep-ocean-exploration::future-ocean.summary:
Future explorers may use smarter robots and better sensors.
Much of the ocean is still unknown.

powerUp.title: Future Thinking
powerUp.text: Separate evidence from prediction.

deep-ocean-exploration::ocean-network.title: A Network of Ocean Sensors
deep-ocean-exploration::ocean-network.summary:
Scientists may place connected sensors across vast areas.
Data could arrive in real time.
deep-ocean-exploration::ocean-network.consequences: Could improve understanding of the ocean.

powerUp.title: Systems Thinking
powerUp.text: Large networks reveal patterns.

deep-ocean-exploration::future-habitats.title: Living Underwater?
deep-ocean-exploration::future-habitats.summary:
Future habitats might support longer stays below the sea.
Many engineering challenges remain.

powerUp.title: Feasibility Check
powerUp.text: Ask what would be needed to make an idea real.

deep-ocean-exploration::alternate-no-scu.title: What If Scuba Had Never Been Invented?
deep-ocean-exploration::alternate-no-scu.summary:
Ocean science might have advanced more slowly.
Fewer people could have explored reefs and shallow seas.
deep-ocean-exploration::alternate-no-scu.consequences: Shows the importance of portable diving gear.

powerUp.title: What-If Lens
powerUp.text: Explore alternate paths without changing evidence.

deep-ocean-exploration::alternate-no-tri.title: What If Nobody Reached Challenger Deep?
deep-ocean-exploration::alternate-no-tri.summary:
The deepest ocean might have remained more mysterious for longer.
Technology could have progressed more slowly.
deep-ocean-exploration::alternate-no-tri.consequences: Highlights the value of landmark expeditions.

powerUp.title: Counterfactual Thinking
powerUp.text: Compare what happened with what might have happened.
